hyperedges.label: Shared Layout Shell (Navbar + Footer + FloatingWhatsApp in RootLayout)

nodes.label: Next.js 16 App Router
nodes.rationale: Server Components by default; Client components isolated to specific interactive elements (mobile drawer, filter tabs, FAQ accordion, floating WhatsApp button) to minimise client bundle.

nodes.label: WhatsApp CTA (Primary Contact Channel)
nodes.rationale: WhatsApp is the primary CTA because local Bangladeshi small businesses predominantly communicate via WhatsApp; lower friction than email or web forms.

nodes.label: CSS Keyframe Animations (Hero Only)
nodes.rationale: Hero section uses CSS keyframes only (no Framer Motion) for GPU-composited animations (transform + opacity); zero JS on animation path for performance.

nodes.label: Next.js Server Actions (Contact Form)
nodes.rationale: Server Actions used for contact form to avoid adding an extra library in v1; logs to console, email integration deferred.

nodes.label: Bilingual Bengali/English Content Strategy
nodes.rationale: Bengali copy targets local Bangladeshi SMB owners who are more comfortable in Bengali; English subtitles maintain professionalism and SEO coverage.

nodes.label: Brand Color System
nodes.rationale: Deep green (#1A4731 / #0D2818) conveys trust and professionalism for local businesses; gold (#F59E0B) used exclusively for primary CTAs and highlights to draw attention to WhatsApp conversion action.

nodes.label: Typography System (Hind Siliguri + Geist)
nodes.rationale: Hind Siliguri chosen for Bengali copy because it offers best readability on Bangladeshi mobile devices; Geist Sans for English to match Next.js project default.

nodes.label: Logo Design (ZeroD text mark)
nodes.rationale: SVG text mark rather than image file: D enclosed in gold circle suggests zero-to-digital loop; dark/light variant via prop avoids separate asset files.

nodes.label: Hero Animations Design
nodes.rationale: Four CSS-only animations: staggered fade-up, breathing gradient, floating ring, and WhatsApp pulse glow. GPU-composited (transform + opacity only) — no JS execution on animation path, no external library.

nodes.label: Tech Stack Decisions
nodes.rationale: Next.js 16 App Router already set up; Tailwind v4 + CSS variables already configured; shadcn/ui partially installed; lucide-react already a dependency; Server Actions for forms avoids extra library in v1.

nodes.label: Vercel Logo
nodes.description: Vercel triangle logo mark rendered as a white upward-pointing triangle on a transparent background. Minimal SVG icon with a single filled path, no text, monochrome white fill. Used as a brand asset in Next.js projects generated via Create Next App.

links.description: The SVG icon visually represents a generic document or file, shown with a folded corner and horizontal lines suggesting text content. It uses a neutral gray (#666) fill in a 16x16 viewBox, typical of UI icon sets for file browsers or upload interfaces.

links.context: Flat vector icon, monochrome gray (#666), fill-rule evenodd, 16x16 viewBox, no stroke. Suitable for small UI usage such as navigation or locale indicators.

links.description: Placed in the public/ directory for use as a static brand/logo asset in a Next.js web application
SVG icon depicts a browser window with a title bar containing three circular dots (traffic light buttons) and a rectangular body, representing a desktop or browser window UI element
Window icon is part of the public static assets UI icon set used in the Next.js project, alongside other icons like file.svg, globe.svg, and vercel.svg
